data.note: Run 1 gradual OOM — avg misses ~496MB peak
Multiple OOMs in 14:14-14:15 — still shows max=1

restart_count.key_finding: RestartCount shows '1' even after 5+ OOM kills. The metric tracks container restarts, not worker restarts. Since gunicorn absorbed the OOM internally, container never actually restarted.

replica_api.note: Container start time unchanged after all 5 OOM kills — proves the container itself never restarted

system_logs.key_finding: ZERO system log entries after 14:06:53Z despite 5 OOM kills. ContainerAppSystemLogs_CL captured initial deployment only.

console_logs.message_format: [ERROR] Worker (pid:N) was sent SIGKILL! Perhaps out of memory?
console_logs.source: gunicorn master process logging to stdout
console_logs.key_finding: Console logs are the ONLY place where OOM evidence exists. The gunicorn master process logs SIGKILL, but this is application-level logging — not platform-level.
